user.title: E.ON Energy
user.description:
Paste your token data from the E.ON Energy website.

How to get it:
1. Log in at www.eonenergy.com in Chrome
2. Open DevTools → Application → Local Storage → https://www.eonenergy.com
3. Find the key starting with **@@auth0spajs@@** and copy its full JSON value

Alternatively, paste just the **id_token** JWT string.

The **fetch day** is the day of month the integration will call the API to refresh data. Tokens expire after ~10 hours, so choose a day when you can log in and re-authenticate if prompted. Billing periods typically close at the end of each month — day 5–10 is a safe default.

reauth_confirm.title: Re-authenticate E.ON Energy
reauth_confirm.description: Your E.ON Energy token has expired. Log in at www.eonenergy.com, then copy fresh token data from DevTools → Application → Local Storage → @@auth0spajs@@ key.

error.invalid_auth: Token rejected or expired. Make sure you copied the full JSON value or a valid id_token JWT.
error.cannot_connect: Unable to reach the E.ON Energy service. Check your internet connection and try again.
error.unknown: An unexpected error occurred. Check the Home Assistant logs for details.

abort.already_configured: E.ON Energy account is already configured.

init.title: E.ON Energy options
init.description: Choose which day of each month the integration should fetch updated consumption data from the API. On all other days it serves the last cached reading without contacting E.ON or raising any errors.
